document.source_title: In IPL 2026, home advantage is a pipe dream

claims.claim_text: Home advantage is no longer universal in the IPL.
claims.evidence_text: The broader numbers from the last two IPL seasons reveal an increasingly inconsistent picture across venues.

entities.name: Delhi Capitals
entities.description: A franchise cricket team based in Delhi.
entities.evidence_text: Delhi Capitals coach Hemang Badani did not intend to make a sweeping statement about the Indian Premier League.

entities.name: Chennai Super Kings
entities.description: A franchise cricket team based in Chennai.
entities.evidence_text: Chennai Super Kings, once nearly unbeatable at Chepauk, won only one game out of five matches in 2025.

entities.name: Mumbai Indians
entities.description: A franchise cricket team based in Mumbai.
entities.evidence_text: Mumbai Indians continue to be formidable at the Wankhede.

entities.name: Rajasthan Royals
entities.description: A franchise cricket team based in Jaipur.
entities.evidence_text: Rajasthan Royals have similarly struggled in Jaipur, winning none of their three matches there.

entities.name: Lucknow Super Giants
entities.description: A franchise cricket team based in Lucknow.
entities.evidence_text: Lucknow Super Giants have found little comfort at the Ekana either.

entities.name: Gujarat Titans
entities.description: A franchise cricket team based in Ahmedabad.
entities.evidence_text: Gujarat Titans have enjoyed a strong record in Ahmedabad.

entities.name: Royal Challengers Bengaluru
entities.description: A franchise cricket team based in Bengaluru.
entities.evidence_text: Royal Challengers Bengaluru have improved at the Chinnaswamy.

metrics.name: Delhi Capitals home wins
metrics.evidence_text: Across the last two seasons, they have managed only three wins in 12 matches at the Kotla.

metrics.name: Delhi Capitals away wins
metrics.evidence_text: The Capitals have won four of six away games this season.

metrics.name: Chennai Super Kings 2025 wins
metrics.evidence_text: Chennai Super Kings, once nearly unbeatable at Chepauk, won only one game out of five matches in 2025.

metrics.name: Chennai Super Kings 2026 wins
metrics.evidence_text: Chennai Super Kings won four out of six in 2026.

citations.title: In IPL 2026, home advantage is a pipe dream

source_credibility.reason: The article provides statistical evidence and expert opinions regarding the changing dynamics of home advantage in IPL.
